title: D002: Implement OpenBao deployment and secret management
summary: Review #8 feedback addressed: (1) `check_initialized()` in init-unseal.sh now guards against empty responses from connectivity failures before parsing JSON, and (2) eso-management.hcl wildcard narrowed from `openstack/*` to `openstack/keystone/*` to enforce least-privilege. Documentation updated to match. Two new review patterns persisted.

tests_checklist.item: Bootstrap scripts are not unit-testable (kubectl/bao exec in live cluster)
Idempotency guards remain intact after changes

code_quality_checklist.item: Changes are minimal and scoped to reviewer feedback
DEVIATION comment updated to reflect narrowed path

security_checklist.item: eso-management policy scoped to narrowest required path (openstack/keystone/*)
All 3 management ExternalSecrets (keystone-db, keystone-admin, mariadb-root-password) still covered by policy paths
No secrets exposed in logs or process arguments

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible

fail_fast_checklist.item: Connectivity failure detected early with clear diagnostic before proceeding to initialization

defensive_checklist.item: Empty response from kube_exec produces actionable error message and exits

suggested_improvements: The existing review pattern file .planwerk/review_patterns/cross-reference-access-policies-against-all-consumers.md (line 21) still references the old fix text 'Added path kv-v2/data/openstack/*' — consider updating it to reflect the narrowed path 'kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/*' for accuracy (out of scope for this diff)
